steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The previous sentence establishes that not all conifers are evergreen. The blank sentence must introduce larch trees as a specific case supporting that claim, so the transition must signal example, not contrast, addition, or simultaneity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘for instance,’: signals that the sentence is an example of the preceding claim. Larch trees exemplify the non-evergreen conifers just mentioned. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘nevertheless,’: signals concession or contrast—that the new information is true despite the previous claim. Larch trees support the claim, not contradict it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘meanwhile,’: signals that two things are happening simultaneously or in parallel. No parallel timeline exists here; the larch sentence illustrates the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘in addition,’: signals a new, separate fact being added. Larch trees are not an additional fact; they are a specific instance of the exception already stated. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
